title: malus transitoria
intro: Malus transitoria, also known as the transitional crabapple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is primarily valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. The plant's leaves and fruits have been used in traditional medicine to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory problems. Malus transitoria can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its benefits to be harnessed effectively.
